prs.title: Skip emitFile() in astro:scripts plugin during serve mode
prs.body:
## Changes

- Guards the `buildStart` hook in the `astro:scripts` Vite plugin to skip `this.emitFile()` when Vite is running in serve mode (`astro dev`).
- `emitFile()` is a Rollup build-time API that Vite stubs out during dev, producing a spurious warning:
  ```
  [WARN] [vite] [plugin:astro:scripts] context method emitFile() is not supported in serve mode.
  ```
- The fix captures the Vite command via the `config` hook and returns early from `buildStart` when `command === 'serve'`. The `emitFile()` call still runs during `build` as intended, ensuring before-hydration script chunks are correctly emitted for production builds.

Closes #16026
Closes #15975

## Testing

- **`before-hydration.test.js`** — All 7 tests pass, covering both SSG and SSR scenarios, in dev and build modes, with and without hydration scripts injected by integrations. This validates that the before-hydration chunk is still correctly emitted during builds while the warning is suppressed during dev.
- **`astro-scripts.test.js`** — All 11 tests pass, covering script rendering, hoisting, inlining, and `injectScript` in both dev and build modes.

## Docs

- No docs update needed — this is a bug fix removing a spurious warning, with no change to user-facing APIs or behavior.

prs.title: MM-59260: Allow permanent deleting a Direct and Group Message

labels.name: release-note
labels.description: Denotes a PR that will be considered when it comes time to generate release notes.

prs.body:
Fixes https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/issues/27489

Currently `deleteChannel` function in `api4/channel.go` and `api4/channel_local.go` doesn’t allow deleting a Direct Message Channel and Group Type Channel.

This PR allows permanently deleting both channel types.
User must have permission `PermissionDeletePrivateChannel` for deletions.

NOTE: These channels are only allowed to be permanently deleted and not soft-deleted/archived.

```release-note
Allowed permanent deletion of Direct Message and Group channels via the API for users with the appropriate permissions.
```

prs.title: [DOC] Add missing YUIDoc tags for @ember/helper exports
prs.body: Resolves #20153. Adding missing `@method`, `@for`, `@public`, and `@static` tags for `array`, `concat`, `fn`, `get`, `hash`, and `uniqueId` in `@ember/helper/index.ts` so they appear in the documentation.

prs.title: [BUGFIX] Fix type-check errors in router_js and integration-tests
prs.body: This PR fixes a type-check error causing tsc --noEmit to fail by adding missing types for js-reporters.

prs.title: [BUGFIX] Fix EmberArray.reduce to match native behavior
prs.body:
## Description
Fixes a FIXME where `EmberArray#reduce` did not match the native `Array.prototype.reduce` behavior when no `initialValue` was provided. See `packages/@ember/array/index.ts`.

- If no `initialValue` is provided and the array is empty, it now throws a `TypeError` (consistent with native).
- If no `initialValue` is provided, it uses the first item as the initial value and begins iterating from index 1.

Tested with new test cases in `reduce-test.js`.

## Verification
`pnpm lint:format:fix` and `pnpm test` were run to ensure formatting is correct and no regressions were introduced.

prs.title: [CLEANUP] Fix missing ApplicationInstance in Router tests
prs.body:
This PR removes a workaround in `packages/@ember/routing/router.ts` that allowed `ApplicationInstance` to be missing in tests. This occurred because `RouterNonApplicationTestCase` in `packages/internal-test-helpers` did not register an application instance.

I have updated the test case to register a mock `ApplicationInstance`, allowing the router to enforce strict dependency checks and removing the `TODO(SAFETY)` comment.

This ensures better type safety and correctness in the router's dependencies.

prs.title: [CLEANUP beta] Fix missing ApplicationInstance in Router tests
prs.body:
This PR removes a workaround in `packages/@ember/routing/router.ts` that allowed `ApplicationInstance` to be missing in tests. This occurred because `RouterNonApplicationTestCase` in `packages/internal-test-helpers` did not register an application instance.

I have updated the test case to register a mock `ApplicationInstance`, allowing the router to enforce strict dependency checks and removing the `TODO(SAFETY)` comment.

This ensures better type safety and correctness in the router's dependencies.

prs.title: Examples: Improve WebXR HTTPS requirement message
prs.body:
Title: Examples: Improve WebXR HTTPS requirement message

Description: The WebXR buttons (ARButton, VRButton, XRButton) displayed the message "WEBXR NEEDS HTTPS" when the context was not secure. This phrasing could be perceived as aggressive or "yelling".

Fixed by changing the message to "HTTPS REQUIRED", which conveys the same information in a more professional tone.

Related issue: N/A

prs.title: fix(engine-javascript): resolve regex engine limitations and discrepa…
prs.body:
This PR addresses critical regressions and discrepancies in the engine-javascript package where it failed to match the behavior of the WASM engine, particularly for complex grammars like SQL.

What this PR solves:

Fixes SQL Grammar Failures: Resolves issues where large regex patterns (e.g., extensive keyword alternations) caused V8 regex engine limitations or incorrect matches.
Corrects Capture Group Indexing: Implements logic to correctly handle capture groups when patterns are split. Previously, splitting large patterns caused "Expected X groups, received Y" errors.
Fixes Regex Flag Incompatibility: Resolves a specific issue where the default v flag (ES2024) caused faulty emulation of word boundaries (\b) when combined with case-insensitivity ((?i)).

prs.title: [BUGFIX] Fix 'on' modifier error message regression
prs.body:
This PR fixes a regression where the helpful error message for the 'on' modifier was lost when the callback is undefined or not a function.

It adds an explicit runtime check in DEBUG mode to ensure the callback is a function, and includes a reproduction test case to verify the fix.

prs.title: Material: Fix copy method to include allowOverride property
prs.body:
The copy method in Material was missing the allowOverride property that is defined in the constructor. When cloning a Material instance, this property was not copied to the new material, causing incorrect behavior when allowOverride was set to false.

Fixed by ensuring allowOverride is properly copied in the copy method.

Related issue: N/A

**Description**

The copy method in Material was missing the allowOverride property that is defined in the constructor. When cloning a Material instance, this property was not copied to the new material, causing incorrect behavior when allowOverride was set to false.

prs.title: docs(auth): Clarify Google SSO username/email sync behavior

labels.name: 4: Reviews Complete
labels.description: All reviewers have approved the pull request

labels.name: Changelog/Done
labels.description: Required changelog entry has been written

labels.name: release-note-none
labels.description: Denotes a PR that doesn't merit a release note.

prs.body:
Summary
This PR adds a clarification note in Security Settings → Sign-in Method explaining that Google SSO does not automatically sync username or email changes from the identity provider (Google Workspace) back to Mattermost.

Currently, if a user’s Google name or email is corrected in Google Workspace, Mattermost does not update the username automatically. The only way to update it is:

• Temporarily switch the account to email/password login  
• Update the username  
• Switch back to Google SSO  

This behavior was unclear in the UI, so a documentation note has been added to help admins/users understand the limitation.

Why This Is Needed
Issue: #34456  
Admins reported confusion when Google Workspace user details were corrected but Mattermost did not reflect the changes.

This PR clarifies the expected behavior to avoid confusion.

Changes
• Added an SSO clarification note under Sign-in Method section  
• No functional logic changed  
• Purely documentation/UI text improvement

Testing
1. Sign in with a Google SSO account.  
2. Navigate to Account Settings → Security → Sign-in Method.  
3. Edit the section and verify that the new clarification message is visible.

```release-note
NONE



<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Added a contextual note in Security Settings that explains how Google SSO can synchronize usernames and emails, shown alongside the Sign-in Method details.

* **Documentation**
  * Added localization strings to surface the new SSO guidance note in the UI.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: Issue 58 auth bridge extension
prs.body:
1.Syncs real time data from extension and dispaly in web app
2.This pull request implements a bridge between the Chrome extension and the main web application to sync logout. When the user logs out from the extension, it logouts in the web app.

prs.title: Implement auth bridge between extension and main app [Issue #58]
prs.body:
implements the authentication bridge between the Chrome extension and the main Screentime web app.

Updates:
- When the user logs into the web app, the token and auth status are saved to `chrome.storage.local`
- popup.js and background.js handle token sync and real-time storage changes
- Ensures that the extension stays in sync with the main app's authentication state

Verified:
- Web app login → token stored → extension shows "Active & Syncing"
- Manual logout → token cleared → extension reverts to "Not Syncing"

prs.title: Add domain activity tracking to backend and frontend
prs.body:
Implemented screen time tracking:

- Backend: Created API to POST and GET domain activity (startTime, endTime, duration)
- Frontend: Automatically tracks time on Dashboard load/unload
- Displays start and end time on the dashboard
- Tested backend endpoints using Postman
#52

prs.title: Add domain tracking and frontend time display
prs.body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Added time tracking for domain usage, including start time, end time, and duration, with data displayed dynamically on the Dashboard.
  * Introduced a "Sign in with Google" option in the navigation bar for unauthenticated users.
  * Implemented backend endpoints and database models to log and retrieve domain activity and time logs.
  * Added a health check endpoint to confirm server status.

* **Chores**
  * Updated and added dependencies to support new features and improve development workflow.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
